perturbation.original_text: 23.O. WAIVER OF JURY TRIAL. BOTH PRETZEL TIME AND THE FRANCHISEE IRREVOCABLY WAIVES TRIAL BY JURY IN ANY ACTION, PROCEEDING OR COUNTERCLAIM, WHETHER AT LAW OR IN EQUITY, BROUGHT BY EITHER PARTY. THE PARTIES FURTHER AGREE THAT NEITHER SHALL DEMAND A JURY TRIAL IN THE EVENT OF LITIGATION.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.B. DEFINITIONS... “Waiver of Jury Trial” - both Pretzel Time and the franchisee may elect to waive the right to a trial by jury in any action, proceeding or counterclaim, whether at law or in equity, brought by either party. The parties agree that a jury trial demand shall be determined by the facts presented to the Court.
perturbation.explanation: The original franchise agreement contains a clear and prominent clause waiving the right to a jury trial. Moving this waiver to the definition section and rephrasing introduces ambiguity and affects its enforceability. The added provision introduces uncertainty, making it harder to enforce the original waiver of jury trial.
perturbation.law_explanation: The modified text contradicts 735 ILCS 5/2-1105 because the statute dictates procedures for jury trials and their waiver, implying a structured legal process. The modification disrupts this by suggesting jury trial demand depends on the 'facts presented to the Court,' thus making the waiver less clear and predictable than the law intends.

perturbation.justification: The modified text creates a contradiction by introducing ambiguity into a previously clear waiver of jury trial. The original text states a firm waiver, while the changed text makes the waiver dependent on the 'facts presented to the Court,' potentially undermining the original intent. The 'Law Explanation' supports that the change makes the waiver less clear and predictable than the law intends. This ambiguity introduces a structural flaw affecting the enforceability of the jury trial waiver. While Scraped Law Snippet 1 mainly discusses updates to statutes, the main point is that laws have been enacted in order to create a structured legal process, and the changed contract undermines this by making jury trial demand reliant on court evaluation, making the original waiver harder to enforce.

perturbation.original_text: 4.I. LATE CHARGE AND INTEREST. To compensate Pretzel Time for the increased administrative expense of handling late payments, Pretzel Time may charge Franchisee a $50.00 late charge for each delinquent payment. All Royalty and Advertising Fund Fees, amounts due for purchases by Franchisee from Pretzel Time or its Affiliates, and other amounts which Franchisee owes to Pretzel Time or its Affiliates shall bear interest after their due date at a rate equal to the lesser of: (1) eighteen percent (18%) per annum for the number of days which such payment is due; or (2) the highest applicable legal rate permitted by applicable law. Franchisee acknowledges that this Section shall not constitute Pretzel Time's or its Affiliates' agreement to accept such payments after they are due or a commitment by Pretzel Time or its Affiliates to extend credit to or otherwise finance operation of the Unit. Notwithstanding the provisions of this Section 4.I., Franchisee acknowledges and agrees that his failure to pay all amounts when due shall constitute grounds for termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 8.E. CONSTRUCTION ASSISTANCE... 8.F. LIMITATION ON LIABILITY. To compensate Pretzel Time for the increased administrative expense of handling late payments, Pretzel Time may charge Franchisee a $50.00 late charge for each delinquent payment. All Royalty and Advertising Fund Fees, amounts due for purchases by Franchisee from Pretzel Time or its Affiliates, and other amounts which Franchisee owes to Pretzel Time or its Affiliates shall bear interest after their due date at a rate equal to the lesser of: (1) eighteen percent (18%) per annum for the number of days which such payment is due; or (2) the highest applicable legal rate permitted by applicable law. Franchisee acknowledges that this Section shall not constitute Pretzel Time's or its Affiliates' agreement to accept such payments after they are due or a commitment by Pretzel Time or its Affiliates to extend credit to or otherwise finance operation of the Unit. Notwithstanding the provisions of this Section 4.I., Franchisee acknowledges and agrees that his failure to pay all amounts when due shall constitute grounds for termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original late charge and interest clause is located within the section outlining franchise and other fees. The relocation of the clause to the section detailing construction assistance would likely make it difficult for the franchisee to locate, as it is not logically related to the construction section of the franchise agreement. This introduces ambiguity and affects its enforceability.
perturbation.law_explanation: The relocation of the late charge and interest clause to the construction assistance section contradicts 16 CFR § 429.1 because the cooling-off rule emphasizes clear and conspicuous disclosure of financial terms and cancellation rights, which would not be met if the late fee disclosure is buried within an unrelated section of the contract.

perturbation.justification: The relocation of the late charge clause to an unrelated section (construction assistance) creates a structural flaw that contradicts the principle of clear disclosure, as emphasized in the Law Explanation referencing 16 CFR § 429.1, which promotes clear disclosure of financial terms. Scraped Law Snippet 1 indicates that 16 CFR Part 429 pertains to trade regulation rules, specifically concerning cooling-off periods, implying the importance of transparent disclosures within sales agreements. The change makes the late charge provision difficult to locate and understand, therefore making it contradict the need for conspicuous disclosure.

perturbation.original_text: 20.G. COVENANT NOT TO COMPETE. Upon termination of this Agreement, in accordance with its terms and conditions or by Franchisee without cause, or upon expiration of this Agreement (unless the Franchise is renewed as provided for in this Agreement), Franchisee and its Owners agree that for a period of TWELVE (12) months commencing on the effective date of termination or expiration or the date on which Franchisee complies with this Section, whichever is later, neither Franchisee, nor its Owners, nor any person or entity affiliated with Franchisee or Franchisee's shareholders or partners shall have any direct or indirect interest (through a member of the immediate families of Franchisee or its Owners or otherwise) as a disclosed or beneficial owner, investor, partner, director, officer, employee, consultant, representative, agent or in any other capacity in any Competitive Business located or operating: (1) at the Site; (2) within three (3) miles of the Unit; and/or (3) within three (3) miles of any other Pretzel Time Unit in operation or under development on the effective date of termination or expiration of this agreement for a period of one year after the termination or expiration.
perturbation.changed_text: 11.A. PROVIDING OF ADVERTISING MATERIALS. Franchisee and Pretzel Time agree and recognize the value of uniform advertising to the goodwill and public image of Pretzel Time Units...Upon termination of this Agreement, in accordance with its terms and conditions or by Franchisee without cause, or upon expiration of this Agreement (unless the Franchise is renewed as provided for in this Agreement), Franchisee and its Owners agree that for a period of TWELVE (12) months commencing on the effective date of termination or expiration or the date on which Franchisee complies with this Section, whichever is later, neither Franchisee, nor its Owners, nor any person or entity affiliated with Franchisee or Franchisee's shareholders or partners shall have any direct or indirect interest (through a member of the immediate families of Franchisee or its Owners or otherwise) as a disclosed or beneficial owner, investor, partner, director, officer, employee, consultant, representative, agent or in any other capacity in any Competitive Business located or operating: (1) at the Site; (2) within three (3) miles of the Unit; and/or (3) within three (3) miles of any other Pretzel Time Unit in operation or under development on the effective date of termination or expiration of this agreement for a period of one year after the termination or expiration.
perturbation.explanation: Moving the covenant not to compete to the section describing advertising materials makes it less visible and harder to find, which is against the legal expectation for such clauses. Covenants not to compete are expected to be presented clearly. This introduces ambiguity and affects its enforceability.
perturbation.law_explanation: Moving the non-compete clause to the advertising section contradicts the spirit of Cal. Bus. & Prof. Code § 16600. While § 16600 generally invalidates non-compete agreements, exceptions exist. Regardless, its enforceability depends on clarity and reasonable placement in the contract, not concealment within a section about advertising. This relocation makes it appear as if it is an advertising-related clause, instead of a restriction of trade.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: California Code, BPC 16600 skip to content home accessibility FAQ feedback sitemap login x Quick Search: Bill Number Bill Keyword Home Bill Information California Law Publications Other Resources My Subscriptions My Favorites California Law >> >> Code Section Code Section Code: Select Code CONS BPC CIV CCP COM CORP EDC ELEC EVID FAM FIN FGC FAC GOV HNC HSC INS LAB MVC PEN PROB PCC PRC PUC RTC SHC UIC VEH WAT WIC Article: Section: Code: Select Code All BPC CIV CCP COM CORP EDC ELEC EVID FAM FIN FGC FAC GOV HNC HSC INS LAB MVC PEN PROB PCC PRC PUC RTC SHC UIC VEH WAT WIC Section: Keyword(s): Code Search Text Search Up^ << Previous Next >> cross-reference chaptered bills PDF | Add To My Favorites Search Phrase: Code Text Business and Professions Code - BPC DIVISION 7. GENERAL BUSINESS REGULATIONS [16000 - 18107] ( Division 7 added by Stats. 1941, Ch. 61. ) PART 2. PRESERVATION AND REGULATION OF COMPETITION [16600 - 17365] ( Part 2 added by Stats. 1941, Ch. 526. ) CHAPTER 1. Contracts in Restraint of Trade [16600 - 16607] ( Chapter 1 added by Stats. 1941, Ch. 526. ) 16600. (a) Except as provided in this chapter, every contract by which anyone is restrained from engaging in a lawful profession, trade, or business of any kind is to that extent void. (b) (1) This section shall be read broadly, in accordance with Edwards v. Arthur Andersen LLP (2008) 44 Cal.4th 937, to void the application of any noncompete agreement in an employment context, or any noncompete clause in an employment contract, no matter how narrowly tailored, that does not satisfy an exception in this chapter. (2) This subdivision does not constitute a change in, but is declaratory of, existing law. (c) This section shall not be limited to contracts where the person being restrained from engaging in a lawful profession, trade, or business is a party to the contract. (Amended by Stats. 2023, Ch. 828, Sec. 1. (AB 1076) Effective January 1, 2024.)
perturbation.justification: The modification introduces a structural flaw by moving the covenant not to compete to a section about advertising materials. This contradicts the principle that such clauses should be clearly presented. Cal. Bus. & Prof. Code § 16600, as detailed in Scraped Law Snippet 1, addresses contracts that restrain trade. While it generally voids such contracts, the enforceability relies on clarity and reasonable placement within the contract. Moving the non-compete clause diminishes its visibility and could be interpreted as an attempt to obscure its purpose, thereby undermining its enforceability and going against the spirit of the law. The structural flaw directly affects the clarity of the obligation, leading to a legal contradiction by making it difficult to locate and interpret.
